texto: Women have been brought up with the false sense that they have all the options in the world. We don’t understand that the culture really isn’t offering us all of these options – there still are very strong pressures to conform. We have to step outside the culture to be able to make choices that will really give us what we want. But we lack the psychic mechanisms to do this, to really choose.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Dalma Heyn sobre temas ligados a amor. A afirmação “Women have been brought up with the false sense that they have all the options in the world. We d…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a amor, dalma-heyn, love, útil em momentos de love e power.

texto: How else to make a dent in an object as immovable as patriarchy itself...?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Dalma Heyn que provoca temas ligados a autoestima. Ao questionar “How else to make a dent in an object as immovable as patriarchy itself...?”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre autoestima, assholes, feminism, com foco em casamento e feminism.

texto: He was too busy checking out and checking in, making and breaking plans, buying and losing cell phones, playing computer games and pool, looking at stock quotes, and living the chaotic life that effectively took up all his energy and time.

texto: [Women's magazines]ignore older women or pretend that they don’t exist; magazines try to avoid photographs of older women, and when they feature celebrities who are over sixty, ‘retouching artists’ conspire to ‘help’ beautiful women look more beautiful, ie less than their age...By now readers have no idea what a real woman’s 60 year old face looks like in print because it’s made to look 45. Worse, 60 year old readers look in the mirror and think they are too old, because they’re comparing themselves to some retouched face smiling back at them from a magazine.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Dalma Heyn: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “[Women's magazines]ignore older women or pretend that they don’t exist; magazines try to avoid ph…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, aging, beauty pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e beauty.

texto: Married women are far more depressed than married men -- in unhappy marriages, three times more; and -- interestingly -- in happy marriages, five times more. In truth, it is men who are thriving in marriage, now as always, and who show symptoms of psychological and physical distress outside it. Not only their emotional well-being but their very lives, some studies say, depend on being married!
explicacao: Citação de Dalma Heyn sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “Married women are far more depressed than married men -- in unhappy marriages, three times more; …” articula reflexao, dalma-heyn, depression em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como casamento e termino.
